interviewStages: Coding (1–2 rounds) — algorithms with practical engineering flavor
System design (1 round) — financial systems with crypto-specific considerations
Domain depth (1 round) — depends on role: trading, blockchain / DeFi, mining infrastructure, asset management
Behavioral / cultural fit (1 round) — collaboration, comfort with crypto chaos, business-line fit

compensation: New-grad SWE: $130k–$200k total comp first year
Senior trader / Senior MD: $1M–$5M+ in good crypto cycles
The diversification across 5 business lines is more substantial than candidates expect — engineers can move between trading, asset management, IB, mining over their career.
The Mike Novogratz culture is more traditional-finance-flavored than candidates expect from a crypto firm.
The Galaxy Helios facility's pivot to AI compute hosting is real and growing — engineers wanting the crypto-AI infrastructure intersection have unique opportunities here.
The NASDAQ listing (2025) provides more financial transparency than crypto-native private firms.
Compensation is base + bonus + stock with crypto-cycle volatility; calibrate against multi-year average rather than peak/trough.

Galaxy Digital

Galaxy Digital Interview Guide 2026: Institutional Crypto, Mike Novogratz Era, Trading + Asset Management + Mining + Investment Banking

Galaxy Digital (NASDAQ: GLXY, listed in May 2025 after years of TSX-only listing) is the most diversified institutional crypto firm globally. Founded in 2018 by Mike Novogratz (former Goldman Sachs / Fortress macro trader), Galaxy operates across crypto trading, asset management, mining, investment banking, and venture investing — distinct from pure-play crypto firms like Coinbase (exchange) or Wintermute (market making). The hiring process reflects the firm's hybrid identity: parts feel like a hedge fund, parts like an investment bank, parts like a crypto-native startup. This guide covers what Galaxy does, the engineering tracks, the interview process, and what makes Galaxy hiring distinctive in 2026.

What Galaxy Digital Does

Galaxy operates four primary business lines:

- Galaxy Trading: spot and derivatives trading, OTC market making, lending. Galaxy is one of the largest institutional crypto trading desks. Counterparties include institutional investors, hedge funds, miners, exchanges.

- Galaxy Asset Management: crypto-focused asset management. Active funds, indices, structured products, co-managed funds with traditional asset managers (e.g., Galaxy / Invesco partnership for institutional crypto products).

- Galaxy Investment Banking: M&A advisory, capital markets origination, capital introductions for crypto-related transactions. Distinct from peer crypto firms; closest analog to a traditional investment bank specializing in crypto.

- Galaxy Mining + Infrastructure: Bitcoin mining operations (substantial Helios facility in Texas), staking services for proof-of-stake networks, AI / HPC compute hosting via Galaxy Helios. The mining facilities have been substantially repurposed for AI compute hosting alongside Bitcoin mining.

- Galaxy Ventures: venture capital investing in crypto startups. Smaller than dedicated crypto VCs but real fund.

Distinctive features:

- Diversification across crypto verticals: few firms operate at all five Galaxy lines (trading + asset management + IB + mining + VC). Most peers focus on 1–2 verticals. The diversification creates engineering complexity but also business resilience across crypto cycles.

- NASDAQ listing (May 2025): after years on Toronto Stock Exchange only, Galaxy listed on NASDAQ as GLXY in May 2025. The dual listing brings more US institutional visibility and broader investor base.

- Mike Novogratz era: Novogratz's Goldman Sachs / Fortress pedigree shapes the firm's culture — closer to traditional finance than typical crypto-native firms. Senior staff often have traditional Wall Street backgrounds.

- AI / HPC hosting pivot: Galaxy's Helios facility (originally Bitcoin mining) has been substantially repositioned for AI compute hosting (CoreWeave, others). The pivot reflects the broader trend of crypto miners diversifying into AI infrastructure.

- Public company: NASDAQ: GLXY; substantial financial disclosure transparency unusual among crypto firms.

Roles Galaxy Digital Hires For

Software engineer (trading systems)

Builds the trading infrastructure for Galaxy's institutional trading desk — exchange connectivity, OTC ticket booking, risk systems, settlement. Mix of C++, Python, Go. Smaller team than at HFT firms; focused on institutional rather than HFT use cases.

Quantitative trader / analyst

Trading strategies, market making, OTC pricing. Less algorithmic than HFT firms; more fundamental and macro-flavored given Novogratz heritage.

Software engineer (asset management platform)

Builds infrastructure for Galaxy's asset management products — fund administration, NAV calculation, investor reporting, custody integration. Java + Python heavy.

Software engineer (DeFi / on-chain)

Builds on-chain infrastructure — staking systems, DeFi interactions, multi-chain operations. Smaller team than at DeFi-pure firms but real engineering.

ML engineer / data engineer

Crypto market data analytics, alternative data, risk modeling, ad-hoc trading research. Growing area.

Mining / infrastructure engineer (Galaxy Helios)

Operates the Helios mining + AI compute hosting facility in Texas. Datacenter operations, hardware management, hybrid Bitcoin mining + AI
